stories.title: US and Iran move closer to peace deal as Tehran signals obstacles remain
stories.summary: The top story globally: negotiations between the US and Iran are intensifying, with Trump hinting a deal could reopen the Strait of Hormuz and ease oil supply fears. Iran pushed back saying a deal is 'not imminent,' even as the US secretary of state signaled progress. The diplomacy is the most consequential Middle East development in years.

links.headline: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
links.excerpt: Iran's foreign ministry responded to US optimism by cautioning that significant obstacles remain before any agreement can be finalized.
links.imageAlt: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says

links.headline: Why the Potential Iran Deal May Be Almost as Divisive as the War Itself
links.excerpt: CNN weighs domestic political blowback from both parties over any negotiated settlement with Tehran.
links.imageAlt: Why the Potential Iran Deal May Be Almost as Divisive as the War Itself

links.headline: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
links.excerpt: Trump said the deal would include reopening the Strait of Hormuz, driving the sharpest oil price drop in weeks.
links.imageAlt: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal

stories.imageAlt: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says

stories.title: At least 82 killed in China's deadliest coal mine disaster in over a decade
stories.summary: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Shanxi province killed at least 82 people, with two still missing. On China's tightly-controlled internet, citizens are calling for accountability. It is the country's deadliest mining accident since 2016.

links.headline: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
links.excerpt: BBC reports on the public fury and demands for justice despite China's tightly-controlled internet environment.
links.imageAlt: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years

links.headline: Moment of gas explosion at China coal mine
links.excerpt: Video coverage of the blast with official confirmation of 82 dead and rescue operations ongoing for missing miners.

links.headline: China coal mine disaster: Safety questions mount after 82 killed
links.excerpt: ABC adds international perspective on China's mining safety record and the political fallout from the disaster.

stories.imageAlt: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years

stories.title: Large-scale Russian hypersonic missile attack hits Ukraine, four dead
stories.summary: Russia launched a major overnight attack on Ukraine using a hypersonic missile traveling over 10 times the speed of sound, killing four and wounding dozens. The strikes targeted energy infrastructure amid Ukraine's ongoing deep-drone campaign against Russian refineries.

links.headline: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
links.excerpt: BBC reports details of the hypersonic missile strike and its impact on civilian infrastructure.
links.imageAlt: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured

links.headline: Russia pounds Ukrainian energy grid with hypersonic missiles
links.excerpt: CNN adds the broader context of the ongoing infrastructure war and Ukraine's air defense challenges.

links.headline: Russia strikes Ukraine with hypersonic missiles in major overnight attack
links.excerpt: The Guardian covers the civilian toll and the international response to the escalation.

stories.imageAlt: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured

stories.title: Pope Leo issues landmark encyclical warning of AI risks in 42,300-word document
stories.summary: In his first encyclical 'Magnifica Humanitas,' Pope Leo issued a sweeping moral warning about artificial intelligence, calling for ethical boundaries on autonomous weapons, surveillance, and algorithmic decision-making affecting human dignity.

links.headline: Pope Leo Presents Stark AI Warnings in First Encyclical
links.excerpt: CNN covers the Vatican reaction and global response from tech leaders and ethicists to the encyclical.
links.imageAlt: Pope Leo Presents Stark AI Warnings in First Encyclical

links.headline: Analysis: Why the AI industry is the real winner of the Musk-Altman trial
links.excerpt: Separate BBC analysis tied to AI governance examines how the high-profile trial clarified legal frameworks.
links.imageAlt: Analysis: Why the AI industry is the real winner of the Musk-Altman trial

links.headline: Pope Leo Warns of Risks From A.I. in 42,300-Word Encyclical
links.excerpt: NYT reports on the scope and content of the first papal document dedicated entirely to technology ethics.

stories.imageAlt: Pope Leo Presents Stark AI Warnings in First Encyclical

stories.title: SpaceX launches most powerful rocket ever built: Starship V3 test flight
stories.summary: SpaceX successfully launched the Starship V3 — the largest and most powerful rocket ever built — on a critical test flight. The vehicle is key to NASA's Artemis program and Starlink deployment, marking a milestone toward fully reusable super-heavy lift capability.

links.headline: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight
links.excerpt: BBC reports the launch details, vehicle specs, and what the V3 upgrade means for deep-space ambitions.
links.imageAlt: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight

links.headline: SpaceX Starship V3 launches successfully in Texas
links.excerpt: CNN adds the booster recovery attempt and reaction from NASA and commercial partners.

links.headline: SpaceX launches Starship V3 in landmark test flight
links.excerpt: AP reports on the successful test flight and its importance for NASA's Artemis program and deep-space ambitions.

stories.imageAlt: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight

stories.title: At least 19 hospitalized after 'strong smell' incident at Tokyo's Ginza 6 mall
stories.summary: A man sprayed a substance at an ATM inside the luxury Ginza 6 shopping centre in Tokyo, sending at least 19 people to hospital with breathing difficulties. The suspect was detained.

links.headline: At least 19 people taken to hospital after 'strong smell' reported at Tokyo mall
links.excerpt: BBC reports the basic facts: police investigating the Ginza 6 incident, 19 hospitalized.
links.imageAlt: At least 19 people taken to hospital after 'strong smell' reported at Tokyo mall

links.headline: Tokyo mall evacuated after 'strong smell' sickens nearly 20 people
links.excerpt: CNN adds details about the evacuation and police investigation.

links.headline: Substance sprayed at Tokyo ATM sends 19 to hospital
links.excerpt: NYT provides international coverage of the Ginza 6 mall incident and police investigation.

stories.imageAlt: At least 19 people taken to hospital after 'strong smell' reported at Tokyo mall

stories.title: Venezuelan prisoners clash with security forces in Barinas prison protest
stories.summary: Inmates at a prison in Barinas state, Venezuela, protested over alleged mistreatment, hanging banners from the roof. The prisoners said security forces opened fire on what they insisted was a peaceful protest.

links.headline: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment
links.excerpt: BBC reports the protest, prisoners' accounts of being fired upon, and the government's denial of excessive force.
links.imageAlt: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment

links.headline: Venezuelan prisoners clash with guards in Barinas protest
links.excerpt: AP wire coverage of the prison protest and allegations of excessive force by security forces.

links.headline: Dozens injured in Venezuelan prison protest as security forces crack down
links.excerpt: CNN covers the international reaction and calls for an independent investigation.

stories.imageAlt: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment

stories.title: Leaked BHP documents reveal world's biggest miner backtracked on climate promises
stories.summary: Hundreds of pages of leaked internal BHP documents show how Australia's largest company publicly positioned itself as a climate leader while internally finding reasons to delay environmental action. The Guardian and ABC jointly revealed the documents, showing projects scrapped and diesel truck investments continuing despite stated emission targets.

links.headline: Document leak at Australia's richest company shows how it put off going green
links.excerpt: ABC's investigative report details internal memos and strategy documents showing BHP's climate delay tactics.
links.imageAlt: Document leak at Australia's richest company shows how it put off going green

links.headline: World's biggest miner BHP backtracks on climate action with key projects put on ice
links.excerpt: The Guardian's lead coverage reveals the full extent of the leaked documents and BHP's internal resistance to green transition.
links.imageAlt: World's biggest miner BHP backtracks on climate action with key projects put on ice

stories.imageAlt: Document leak at Australia's richest company shows how it put off going green

stories.title: AI may speed up search for drugs to treat brain conditions
stories.summary: Researchers are deploying AI tools to dramatically accelerate the identification of drug candidates for neurological conditions like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's, potentially cutting years off traditional discovery timelines.

links.headline: AI may speed up search for drugs to treat brain conditions
links.excerpt: BBC reports on how machine learning models are being trained to screen millions of compounds for neurological drugs.
links.imageAlt: AI may speed up search for drugs to treat brain conditions

links.headline: How sunburn inspired a new way to store energy
links.excerpt: Related BBC science piece on molecular solar thermal storage — a potential clean energy breakthrough inspired by sunburn mechanisms.
links.imageAlt: How sunburn inspired a new way to store energy

links.headline: AI drug discovery offers new hope for Alzheimer's and Parkinson's patients
links.excerpt: The Guardian covers the implications for patients and the timeline to clinical trials.

stories.imageAlt: AI may speed up search for drugs to treat brain conditions

stories.title: Waymo pauses robotaxi operations in five US cities after flood failures
stories.summary: Waymo temporarily halted autonomous taxi services in five US cities after multiple vehicles drove into flooded roads during recent storms. The pause highlights limitations of current autonomous systems in adverse weather.

links.headline: Waymo pauses robotaxis in five US cities after cars drive into flooded roads
links.excerpt: BBC reports the pause, the specific flood incidents, and Waymo's plan for remediation.
links.imageAlt: Waymo pauses robotaxis in five US cities after cars drive into flooded roads

links.headline: Waymo suspends robotaxi service after autonomous vehicles stranded in floodwaters
links.excerpt: CNN covers the scope of the operational pause and regulator responses.

links.headline: Waymo halts robotaxis after cars drive into flood water
links.excerpt: The Guardian adds consumer safety context and competitor positioning in the autonomous vehicle space.

stories.imageAlt: Waymo pauses robotaxis in five US cities after cars drive into flooded roads

stories.title: NACC chief Paul Brereton resigns amid pressure over defence ties and misconduct
stories.summary: The head of Australia's National Anti-Corruption Commission resigned, saying criticism was 'drawing attention away' from the agency's mission. The resignation follows questions about his defence-force connections.

links.headline: NACC boss Paul Brereton resigns amid pressure over misconduct, defence ties
links.excerpt: ABC breaks the story with details of the resignation letter and succession plan.

links.headline: NACC commissioner Brereton quits
links.excerpt: SMH focuses on the defence connections that prompted the resignation.
links.imageAlt: NACC commissioner Brereton quits

stories.title: Bondi terror attack royal commission hears 11 people shot in 30 seconds
stories.summary: The royal commission into the Bondi terror attack heard evidence that gunmen shot 11 people within half a minute of opening fire. Separately, ASIO reviewed past terror cases in 2024 but failed to re-examine the Bondi attackers, raising oversight questions.

links.headline: Bondi beach gunmen shot 11 people within 30 seconds of opening fire, royal commission hears
links.excerpt: The Guardian reports the commission's detailed timeline and the rapid casualty rate.
links.imageAlt: Bondi beach gunmen shot 11 people within 30 seconds of opening fire, royal commission hears

links.headline: ASIO reviewed past terror cases in 2024 but didn't re-examine Bondi attackers
links.excerpt: ABC reports the oversight gap identified by the commission.
links.imageAlt: ASIO reviewed past terror cases in 2024 but didn't re-examine Bondi attackers

links.headline: Bondi commission: 11 shot in 30 seconds, police had 3 minutes' notice
links.excerpt: SMH adds the police response timeline and advance warning details.

stories.imageAlt: Bondi beach gunmen shot 11 people within 30 seconds of opening fire, royal commission hears

stories.title: Zali Steggall's WhatsApp hacked in suspected Russian-linked phishing attack
stories.summary: Independent MP Zali Steggall had her WhatsApp account compromised in March by suspected Russian-linked phishing. The breach led to WhatsApp being blocked on federal parliamentary laptops.

links.headline: Zali Steggall's WhatsApp hacked in suspected Russian attack
links.excerpt: SMH breaks the story with details of the March compromise and the parliamentary security response.
links.imageAlt: Zali Steggall's WhatsApp hacked in suspected Russian attack

links.headline: Zali Steggall's WhatsApp hacked — Teals reportedly forming own party
links.excerpt: The Guardian reports on both the hacking incident and Steggall's simultaneous involvement in Teal party formation talks.

stories.title: Albanese forges ahead with CGT and negative gearing plan as Labor battles One Nation surge
stories.summary: PM Albanese confirmed he will present changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax to parliament on Thursday with possible business carve-outs. Meanwhile Labor issued an urgent donation appeal to counter rising One Nation support.

links.headline: Albanese forges ahead with CGT and negative gearing plan while flagging possible business carve-outs
links.excerpt: The Guardian covers the PM's parliamentary agenda and potential business exemptions.

links.excerpt: SMH ties together the two political stories — Labor fighting on two fronts.

stories.title: Australian astronaut offered place on breakthrough International Space Station mission
stories.summary: Australia's first astronaut has been offered a place on a breakthrough ISS mission, announced during a Western Australia visit by space agency officials. The selection is a milestone for Australia's civilian space program.

links.excerpt: ABC reports the offer, the astronaut's background, and mission timeline.

stories.title: Lacson tells Dela Rosa to 'hide well' as DOJ declares senator a fugitive
stories.summary: Former senator Panfilo Lacson advised Senator Bato Dela Rosa to 'hide well' after the DOJ formally declared him a fugitive and warned his protectors could face legal raps. The escalation increases legal pressure on the former police chief linked to the drug war.

links.headline: Lacson repeats earlier advice for Dela Rosa: Hide well
links.excerpt: Inquirer reports Lacson's statement and the DOJ's harboring warning.

links.headline: DOJ declares Dela Rosa a fugitive, warns protectors
links.excerpt: Rappler covers the legal mechanics of the declaration and ICC implications.

links.headline: Dela Rosa declared fugitive by DOJ; Lacson gives advice
links.excerpt: ABS-CBN delivers the Philippine broadcaster's coverage of the story.

stories.title: Senate leadership battle intensifies as Sara Duterte impeachment trial preparations accelerate
stories.summary: A coalition is reportedly being formed to install Sherwin Gatchalian as Senate President. Separately, the Sara Duterte impeachment prosecution has engaged financial experts. Francis Tolentino was named acting DOLE secretary.

links.headline: Minority bloc pushes for Win as 'coup' talk persists
links.excerpt: Inquirer reports the Senate maneuvering and competing factions.

links.headline: Francis Tolentino appointed acting labor secretary
links.excerpt: Rappler covers the political calculations behind the Tolentino appointment.
links.imageAlt: Francis Tolentino appointed acting labor secretary

links.excerpt: Inquirer reports on forensic accounting preparation for the impeachment trial.
